methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Vital Lyfe is a seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Jonathan Criss and Andrew Harner that has raised $24M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited comp universe of one identifiable private peer (Matic Robotics) and no public comparables in the consumer tech sector, reflecting the early-stage and frontier nature of the business.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method given data availability, applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $24M raised as a valuation proxy, implying a $60M post-money valuation. This multiplier reflects typical step-up dynamics from seed to subsequent funding rounds in consumer tech. The analysis produces a triangulated range of $51M (low) to $69M (high), with $60M as the median estimate.

The single-method foundation and minimal comp visibility are material constraints on conviction. The 2.5x step-up assumption depends heavily on execution risk and market receptivity at the next funding cycle—factors difficult to calibrate from seed-stage financials alone. A meaningful valuation refinement would require either additional private peer data points or forward-looking metrics (user growth, unit economics, retention) that are not present in the current dataset. The $51M–$69M range should be treated as directional rather than definitive.
